Empires & Puzzles Meg Giry Review: The Starlet's Deceptive Healing

Meg Giry brings a standing ovation to sustainability with a massive 60% heal and a nasty trick for enemies. But with stats from 2024, can this Opera star still hold the stage against modern monsters? We review her unique ailment-locking mechanic and current viability.
Meg Giry: The Starlet Ballerina's Deceptive Healing
Meg Giry, The Starlet Ballerina: A Standing Ovation for Sustainability?
Meg Giry is a 5-star Legendary Holy hero from the
event. Originally released in March 2024, she arrived as a pure support healer designed to punish ailment-heavy teams. She's a

, which fits her role perfectly, making her surprisingly durable despite her aging stats. While she doesn't have a costume yet, her base form received a significant mechanics update shortly after release, changing her from a simple cleanser to a tactical "ailment locker."

Meg Giry sports a classic ballerina aesthetic, complete with the tutu and poise, but with a slightly mischievous expression that hints at her "Deceptive Smalltalk." The art is bright and elegant, a sharp contrast to the darker, moodier themes of her Opera colleagues like the Phantom or Odile. She looks like she's about to curtsy right after poisoning your wine.
Let's be real: Meg Giry is a 2024 hero living in a 2026 world. Her base power of

These were monstrous numbers upon release. Today, they are merely "good." Without Limit Breaks (LB2), she will feel fragile against the 600%+ snipers running rampant in wars. However, her Paladin class helps bridge that gap significantly.
Meg Giry's special skill is a mix of brute-force sustainability and subtle control.

This is still one of the highest raw healing percentages in the game. It’s not boosted health (overheal), which is a slight drawback, but recovering 60% of a modern hero's 3000+ HP pool is a massive swing.
She doesn't just cleanse; she takes the
status ailment from all allies and tosses it onto a random enemy. Then, she makes

This is devastating against teams that rely on cleansing (like Toon Vivica). If you pair Meg with a heavy DoT dealer (like a burn or poison hero), and then fire Meg, the enemy is stuck with that damage for the full duration.
A standard but very effective buff. It makes your team incredibly hard to kill with tile damage or slash attacks for 4 turns.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Opera family is known for its "dance" mechanics, but Meg Giry plays a more supportive role.
The Opera bonus gives raw stats (Attack/Defense/HP). It's a nice passive boost, especially if you pair her with another Opera hero (like Odile or Phantom), but she functions perfectly fine solo.
. It means she cannot be Defense Downed by the likes of classic meta tanks. She stays tanky.
when an ailment leaves her. This is incredible self-sustain. If an enemy fires a "cleanse all" or if a DoT expires, she gets a free mini-heal. It makes her deceptively hard to finish off.
. At the start of the battle, she gets a defense boost. Combined with her Paladin class and Passive resistance, she is very sturdy in the opening turns, giving you time to find those yellow tiles.

Upon release, Meg was initially viewed as "meh" because players compared her to overhealers like Cleopatra. However, after her buff (where she started locking ailments), she found a niche in "torture" teams.
Her stats have aged. A 1079 power hero simply takes more damage from modern 1300+ power nukes. She needs full investment (LB2) to survive in top-tier wars.
. She isn't the universal "slot-in" healer she might have been in 2024. However, in

tournaments, she is a nightmare to face. Her "Uncleansable" mechanic remains unique and hasn't been widely replicated, keeping her relevant for offensive strategies that rely on sticking permanent debuffs.
As a Healer and Tank/Flank option, durability is everything.
Maximizes her survival. The Paladin talent triggers off defense, and her percentage-based heal is fine regardless of her Attack stat.
Her tile damage will be negligible.
. There is zero reason to give Meg Giry attack nodes. She exists to stay alive and keep your team alive.
60% recovery is often a full heal for damaged heroes.
Unique utility that prevents enemies from cleansing crucial debuffs.
Paladin class + Defense Ailment Immunity + Defense Buff + Aether Defense means she is extremely physically tough.
Passive healing when ailments expire keeps her alive longer than most healers.
Unlike Hathor or Cleopatra, she cannot boost health above max. This makes her weaker against max-health reduction teams (goblins).
In a fast meta, Average can sometimes be too slow without a good board.
She requires significant resource investment (Alpha Aethers) to match the bulk of modern 5-stars.
Her ailment redistribution hits a random enemy, which is unreliable for strategic targeting.
At Very Fast speed, her 60% heal and defense buff are oppressive.
Specifically when paired with strong DoT heroes (Burn/Poison) to lock those ailments on the defense.
High sustainability makes her great for long, grindy PvE stages.
Meg Giry shines when she isn't just a healer, but an enabler for damage dealers.
Fire your DoT dealer to apply heavy burn/poison. Fire Meg Giry immediately after. The enemy team is now locked with that DoT and cannot cleanse it. Watch them melt while you hide behind +60% defense.
The family bonus boosts their stats significantly. Meg keeps Odile alive while Odile's dance wrecks the enemy.

. A 60% healer will carry you through almost every map stage and raid in the early-to-mid game. Her Paladin class makes her a great tank for your defense team until you get specialized tanks.

- A spicy take from the forums comparing her to Toon Vivica. While Toon Vivica has the immunities, Meg's raw heal percentage is higher, leading to debates on "Sustain vs Utility."
"She’s great against those purple poison titans too."
- Community members noted her Defense Ailment immunity makes her a solid pick against Dark Titans who spam defense down.
It's worth remembering she was considered "meh" at launch. The change to make ailments uncleansable was a direct response to community feedback, turning her from a bench-warmer to a tactical asset.

Meg Giry is the definition of "graceful aging." While her stats aren't stopping the show like they used to, her routine—massive healing mixed with a nasty ailment-locking trick—still deserves applause. She might not be the lead star in the top 100 alliances anymore, but in the right hands, she can still dance circles around unprepared teams.
A solid, reliable healer with a unique offensive twist. Keep her polished for Rush wars.
